basics.title: Grammar Basics
basics.goal: Build a strong foundation for clear and natural spoken English.

topics.title: Talking About People & Things
topics.cando: I can talk about myself and others without repeating names.
topics.definition: Short words that replace nouns to make sentences flow better.
topics.tip: Use these to keep your speech natural and quick.

examples.s: I ordered the pasta, and it arrived quickly.

topics.title: Specifying Amounts
topics.cando: I can clearly indicate quantities of items or people.
topics.definition: Words placed before nouns to specify or indicate amounts.
topics.tip: These make your requests and descriptions precise.

examples.s: Some people prefer tea after meals.
examples.n: 'Some' helps you describe groups without being overly specific.

topics.title: Describing Ownership
topics.cando: I can talk about my things and my relationships.
topics.definition: Words that show who owns or is related to something.
topics.tip: These are essential for talking about your daily life.

examples.n: 'My' clearly links you to the object.

topics.title: Adding Details
topics.cando: I can describe the quality, size, or condition of things.
topics.definition: Words that add detail to nouns.
topics.tip: Use these to make your descriptions more vivid.

examples.n: 'Tasty' describes the quality of the soup.

topics.title: Describing Actions
topics.cando: I can describe when, where, and how things happen.
topics.definition: Words or phrases that add detail to actions.
topics.tip: These help you give clear, specific information.

examples.n: 'Calmly' describes how she performed the action.

topics.title: Naming Things
topics.cando: I can name people, places, and things in my life.
topics.definition: Words for people, places, things, or ideas.
topics.tip: Clear naming helps others understand exactly what you mean.

topics.title: Expressing Actions
topics.cando: I can describe what people are doing or how they feel.
topics.definition: Words that express actions or states of being.
topics.tip: Verbs are the engine of every sentence.

examples.n: 'Enjoy' expresses your feeling toward an action.

beginner.title: Beginner Grammar
beginner.goal: Speak about your daily life with confidence.

topics.title: Talking About Routines
topics.cando: I can describe my daily schedule and habits.
topics.definition: The way we talk about things that happen regularly.
topics.tip: Perfect for talking about your job or school life.

examples.n: This describes a regular morning habit.

topics.title: Talking About Current Events
topics.cando: I can describe what is happening right now.
topics.definition: Used for actions happening at this exact moment.
topics.tip: Use this to update people on what you are doing.

examples.s: I am waiting for my order.
examples.n: Describes an action in progress now.

topics.title: Giving Locations
topics.cando: I can describe where things and people are located.
topics.definition: Words that show the position of one thing to another.
topics.tip: Crucial for giving directions or finding items.

examples.s: The café is near the station.

topics.title: Defining Objects
topics.cando: I can refer to specific or general objects clearly.
topics.definition: Small words used to point to nouns.
topics.tip: Using 'a' or 'the' correctly makes you sound fluent.

topics.title: Giving Instructions
topics.cando: I can give simple commands, tips, or requests.
topics.definition: Sentences used to tell someone what to do.
topics.tip: Great for work or school collaborations.

topics.title: Asking Simple Questions
topics.cando: I can ask basic questions to get information.
topics.definition: Questions that require a 'yes' or 'no' answer.
topics.tip: The easiest way to start a conversation.

examples.n: A quick way to check a preference.

topics.title: Describing Availability
topics.cando: I can talk about what exists in a space.
topics.definition: Used to say that something exists or is available.
topics.tip: Very useful for describing new places.

examples.n: Confirms the existence of a place.

intermediate.title: Intermediate Grammar
intermediate.goal: Use grammar accurately in real-world conversations.

topics.title: Talking About the Past
topics.cando: I can describe things I finished doing.
topics.definition: Used for actions that happened and ended in the past.
topics.tip: Use this for telling stories or sharing past experiences.

topics.title: Setting the Scene
topics.cando: I can describe what was happening at a specific time.
topics.definition: Used to describe a past action in progress.
topics.tip: Good for explaining background events.

examples.s: I was cooking when you called.
examples.n: Sets the scene for an interruption.

topics.title: Discussing Experiences
topics.cando: I can talk about experiences without needing a specific time.
topics.definition: Used to connect the past to the present.
topics.tip: Great for talking about life achievements or tasks finished.

topics.title: Making Comparisons
topics.cando: I can compare people, places, and things.
topics.definition: Used to see which is better, faster, or different.
topics.tip: Helpful when giving your opinion or making choices.

topics.title: Giving Advice & Rules
topics.cando: I can express ability, rules, or give suggestions.
topics.definition: Small verbs that add meaning like 'can' or 'must'.
topics.tip: These are key for professional communication.

topics.title: Getting Details
topics.cando: I can ask for detailed information using wh- words.
topics.definition: Questions using who, what, where, why, and how.
topics.tip: Essential for understanding a situation fully.

topics.title: Talking About Outcomes
topics.cando: I can explain results based on certain conditions.
topics.definition: Used to describe what happens if something else happens.
topics.tip: Excellent for making plans or setting expectations.

examples.s: If it rains, we stay inside.
